017package org.apache.juneau.commons.bean;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.BeanMeta.MethodType.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.AnnotationTraversal.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ReflectionUtils.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
023import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
024import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
025import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
026import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
027
028import java.beans.*;
029import java.io.*;
030import java.lang.reflect.*;
031import java.util.*;
032import java.util.function.*;
033
034import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
035import org.apache.juneau.commons.function.*;
036import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
037import org.apache.juneau.commons.lang.*;
038import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
039import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.Visibility;
040import org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.*;
041
042/**
043 * Encapsulates all access to the properties of a bean class (like a souped-up {@link BeanInfo}).
044 *
045 * <h5 class='topic'>Description</h5>
046 *
047 * Uses introspection to find all the properties associated with this class.  If the
048 * {@link BeanType @BeanType} annotation is present on the class (or the marshalling-side
049 * {@code @Marshalled} sibling), that information is used to determine the properties on the class.
050 * Otherwise, the {@code BeanInfo} functionality in Java is used to determine the properties on the class.
051 *
052 * <h5 class='topic'>Bean property ordering</h5>
053 *
054 * The order of the properties are as follows:
055 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
056 *    <li>
057 *       If {@link BeanType#properties() @BeanType(properties)} is specified on the class, the order matches the
058 *       list of properties in the annotation.
059 *    <li>
060 *       Otherwise, the order is based on the following:
061 *       <ul>
062 *          <li>Public fields (same order as {@code Class.getFields()}).
063 *          <li>Properties returned by {@code BeanInfo.getPropertyDescriptors()}.
064 *          <li>Non-standard getters/setters with {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} annotation defined on them.
065 *       </ul>
066 * </ul>
067 *
068 * <h5 class='topic'>Thread safety</h5>
069 *
070 * Instances are effectively immutable after construction and are safe for concurrent read access.
071 * Construction is not thread-safe and should be completed before sharing an instance across threads.
072 *
073 *
074 * @param <T> The class type that this metadata applies to.
075 */
076@SuppressWarnings({
077   "java:S115", // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention (e.g., PROP_class)
078   "java:S1200" // Central bean-introspection type; high coupling to annotations/reflect/utils is inherent to its role
079})
080public class BeanMeta<T> {
081
082   // Property name constants
083   private static final String PROP_class = "class";
084   private static final String PROP_properties = "properties";
085
086   /**
087    * Represents the result of creating a BeanMeta, including the bean metadata and any reason why it's not a bean.
088    *
089    * @param <T> The bean type.
090    * @param beanMeta The bean metadata, or <jk>null</jk> if the class is not a bean.
091    * @param notABeanReason The reason why the class is not a bean, or <jk>null</jk> if it is a bean.
092    */
093   public record BeanMetaValue<T>(BeanMeta<T> beanMeta, String notABeanReason) {
094      public Optional<BeanMeta<T>> optBeanMeta() { return opt(beanMeta()); }
095      Optional<String> optNotABeanReason() { return opt(notABeanReason()); }
096   }
097
098   /**
099    * Possible property method types.
100    */
101   enum MethodType {
102      UNKNOWN, GETTER, SETTER, EXTRAKEYS;
103   }
104
105   /*
106    * Temporary getter/setter method struct used for calculating bean methods.
107    */
108   private static class BeanMethod {
109      private String propertyName;
110      private MethodType methodType;
111      private Method method;
112      private ClassInfo type;
113
114      private BeanMethod(String propertyName, MethodType type, Method method) {
115         this.propertyName = propertyName;
116         this.methodType = type;
117         this.method = method;
118         this.type = info(type == SETTER ? method.getParameterTypes()[0] : method.getReturnType());
119      }
120
121      @Override /* Overridden from Object */
122      public String toString() {
123         return method.toString();
124      }
125
126      /*
127       * Returns true if this method matches the class type of the specified property.
128       * Only meant to be used for setters.
129       */
130      private boolean matchesPropertyType(BeanPropertyMeta.Builder b) {
131         if (b == null)
132            return false;
133
134         // Don't do further validation if this is the "*" bean property.
135         if ("*".equals(b.name))
136            return true;
137
138         // Get the bean property type from the getter/field.
139         Class<?> pt = null;  // Uses raw Class<?> here because assignment checks are performed against java.lang.Class.
140         if (nn(b.getter))
141            pt = b.getter.getReturnType().inner();
142         else if (nn(b.field))
143            pt = b.field.inner().getType();
144
145         // Matches if only a setter is defined.
146         if (pt == null)
147            return true;
148
149         // Doesn't match if not same type or super type as getter/field.
150         if (! type.isAssignableFrom(pt))
151            return false;
152
153         // If a setter was previously set, only use this setter if it's a closer
154         // match (e.g. prev type is a superclass of this type).
155         if (b.setter == null)
156            return true;
157
158         return type.isStrictChildOf(b.setter.getParameterTypes().get(0).inner());
159      }
160   }
161
162   /*
163    * Represents a bean constructor with its associated property names.
164    *
165    * @param constructor The constructor information.
166    * @param propertyNames The list of property names that correspond to the constructor parameters.
167    */
168   private record BeanConstructor(Optional<ConstructorInfo> constructor, List<String> args) {}
169
170   /**
171    * Creates a {@link BeanMeta} instance for the specified bean type info.
172    *
173    * <p>
174    * This is the marshalling-side factory entry point.  It attempts to create bean metadata for a class; if the class is
175    * determined to be a bean, the returned {@link BeanMetaValue} carries the {@link BeanMeta} instance and a
176    * <jk>null</jk> reason.  Otherwise it carries <jk>null</jk> for the bean metadata and a non-empty string explaining
177    * why the class is not a bean.
178    *
179    * <p>
180    * The bean filter is resolved internally from the {@code @Marshalled} / {@link BeanType @BeanType} annotations on the
181    * class via {@link BeanMetaInitializer#buildBeanFilter(BeanInfo)}.  See {@link #of(Class, BeanConfigContext)} for
182    * the pure bean-modeling-side entry point that does not require a {@link BeanInfo}.
183    *
184    * <h5 class='section'>Exception handling:</h5>
185    * <p>
186    * If a {@link RuntimeException} is thrown during bean metadata creation, it is caught and the exception message
187    * is returned as the {@code notABeanReason} with <jk>null</jk> for the bean metadata.
188    *
189    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
190    * <p class='bjava'>
191    *    <jc>// Create bean metadata for a class via the marshalling-side context.</jc>
192    *    BeanInfo&lt;Person&gt; <jv>cm</jv> = <jv>marshallingContext</jv>.getClassMeta(Person.<jk>class</jk>);
193    *    BeanMetaHolder&lt;Person&gt; <jv>result</jv> = BeanMeta.<jsm>create</jsm>(<jv>cm</jv>, <jk>null</jk>);
194    *
195    *    <jc>// Check if it's a bean</jc>
196    *    <jk>if</jk> (<jv>result</jv>.beanMeta() != <jk>null</jk>) {
197    *       BeanMeta&lt;Person&gt; <jv>bm</jv> = <jv>result</jv>.beanMeta();
198    *       <jc>// Use the bean metadata...</jc>
199    *    } <jk>else</jk> {
200    *       String <jv>reason</jv> = <jv>result</jv>.notABeanReason();
201    *       <jc>// Handle the case where it's not a bean...</jc>
202    *    }
203    * </p>
204    *
205    * @param <T> The class type.
206    * @param cm The bean type info for the class to create bean metadata for.
207    * @param implClass Optional implementation class info to use when looking for a no-arg constructor.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
208    * @return A {@link BeanMetaValue} containing the bean metadata (if successful) or a reason why it's not a bean.
209    */
210   public static <T> BeanMetaValue<T> create(BeanInfo<T> cm, ClassInfo implClass) {
211      try {
212         var cfg = cm.getBeanConfigContext();
213         var ap = cfg.getAnnotationProvider();
214         var bmi = cfg.getBeanMetaInitializer();
215
216         // Sanity checks first.
217         if (cfg.isNotABean(cm))
218            return notABean("Class matches exclude-class list");
219
220         if (cfg.isBeansRequireSerializable() && ! cm.isAssignableTo(Serializable.class) && ! bmi.hasBeanRegistrationAnnotation(cfg, cm))
221            return notABean("Class is not serializable");
222
223         if (ap.has(BeanIgnore.class, cm))
224            return notABean("Class is annotated with @BeanIgnore");
225
226      if ((! cfg.getBeanClassVisibility().isVisible(cm.getModifiers()) || cm.isAnonymousClass()) && ! bmi.hasBeanRegistrationAnnotation(cfg, cm))
227         return notABean("Class is not public");
228
229         var bm = new BeanMeta<>(cm, bmi.buildBeanFilter(cm), null, implClass);
230
231         if (nn(bm.notABeanReason))
232            return notABean(bm.notABeanReason);
233
234         return new BeanMetaValue<>(bm, null);
235      } catch (RuntimeException e) {
236         return new BeanMetaValue<>(null, e.getMessage());
237      }
238   }
239
240   /*
241    * Extracts the property name from {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} or {@link Name @Name} annotations.
242    *
243    * <p>
244    * If {@link Name @Name} annotations are present, returns the value from the last one.
245    * Otherwise, searches through {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} annotations and returns the first non-empty
246    * {@link BeanProp#value() value()} or {@link BeanProp#name() name()} found.
247    *
248    * @param p List of {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} annotations.
249    * @param n List of {@link Name @Name} annotations.
250    * @return The property name, or <jk>null</jk> if no name is found.
251    */
252   private static String bpName(List<BeanProp> p, List<Name> n) {
253      if (p.isEmpty() && n.isEmpty())
254         return null;
255      if (! n.isEmpty())
256         return last(n).value();
257
258      var name = Holder.of(p.isEmpty() ? null : "");
259      p.forEach(x -> {
260         if (! x.value().isEmpty())
261            name.set(x.value());
262         if (! x.name().isEmpty())
263            name.set(x.name());
264      });
265
266      return name.orElse(null);
267   }
268
269   /*
270    * Extracts the property name from a single {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} or {@link Name @Name} annotation.
271    *
272    * <p>
273    * For {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} annotations, returns the first non-empty value found in the following order:
274    * <ol>
275    *    <li>{@link BeanProp#name() name()}
276    *    <li>{@link BeanProp#value() value()}
277    * </ol>
278    *
279    * <p>
280    * For {@link Name @Name} annotations, returns the {@link Name#value() value()}.
281    *
282    * <p>
283    * This method is used to extract property names from individual annotations, typically when processing
284    * annotation lists in stream operations.
285    *
286    * @param ai The annotation info containing either a {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} or {@link Name @Name} annotation.
287    * @return The property name extracted from the annotation, or <jk>null</jk> if no name is found.
288    * @see #bpName(List, List)
289    */
290   private static String name(AnnotationInfo<?> ai) {
291      if (ai.isType(BeanProp.class)) {
292         var p = ai.cast(BeanProp.class).inner();
293         if (ne(p.name()))
294            return p.name();
295         if (ne(p.value()))
296            return p.value();
297      } else {
298         var n = ai.cast(Name.class).inner();
299         if (ne(n.value()))
300            return n.value();
301      }
302      return null;
303   }
304
305   /*
306    * Shortcut for creating a BeanMetaValue with a not-a-bean reason.
307    */
308   private static <T> BeanMetaValue<T> notABean(String reason) {
309      return new BeanMetaValue<>(null, reason);
310   }
311
312   private BeanConstructor beanConstructor;                                   // The constructor for this bean.
313   private final Object marshallingContext;                                   // MarshallingContext, but Object-typed so the field can live in commons.bean.  Cast to MarshallingContext at marshalling-side use sites.  Null when constructed via {@link #of(Class, BeanConfigContext)}.
314   private final BeanConfigContext config;                                    // Bean-modeling settings facade — always non-null.  Sources: marshallingContext.getBeanConfigContext() (marshalling-side) or the explicit BeanConfigContext (commons-side).
315   private final BeanFilter beanFilter;                                       // Optional bean filter associated with the target class.  Typed as the bean-modeling-side SPI seam; marshalling-side callers narrow back to MarshalledFilter (the only concrete in-tree implementation).
316   private final NullableSupplier<InvocationHandler> beanProxyInvocationHandler;  // The invocation handler for this bean (if it's an interface).
317   private final Supplier<BeanRegistryLookup> beanRegistry;                   // The bean registry for this bean.  Exposed through the BeanRegistryLookup SPI seam.
318   private final Supplier<List<ClassInfo>> classHierarchy;                    // List of all classes traversed in the class hierarchy.
319   private final BeanInfo<T> classMeta;                                       // The target class type that this meta object describes.  Null when constructed via {@link #of(Class, BeanConfigContext)}.  Typed against the bean-modeling SPI seam.
320   private final ClassInfo classInfo;                                         // Pure-reflection view of the bean class that decouples bean modeling from ClassMeta.  Always non-null.
321   private final Supplier<String> dictionaryName;                             // The @Marshalled(typeName) annotation defined on this bean class.
322   private final BeanPropertyMeta dynaProperty;                               // "extras" property.
323   @SuppressWarnings({
324      "rawtypes" // Raw type required at this call site; generic type is verified at runtime
325   })
326   private final Class<? extends BeanFactory> factoryClass;  // @BeanType(factory=X.class) — null means no factory.
327   private final boolean fluentSetters;                                       // Whether fluent setters are enabled.
328   private final Map<Method,String> getterProps;                              // The getter properties on the target class.
329   private final Map<String,BeanPropertyMeta> hiddenProperties;               // The hidden properties on the target class.
330   private final ConstructorInfo implClassConstructor;                        // Optional constructor to use if one cannot be found.
331   private final String notABeanReason;                                       // Readable string explaining why this class wasn't a bean.
332   private final Map<String,BeanPropertyMeta> properties;                     // The properties on the target class.
333   private final Map<Method,String> setterProps;                              // The setter properties on the target class.
334   private final boolean unsortedProperties;                                  // Whether properties should use natural JVM-dependent order.
335   private final ClassInfo stopClass;                                         // The stop class for hierarchy traversal.
336   private final BeanPropertyMeta typeProperty;                               // "_type" mock bean property.
337   private final String typePropertyName;                                     // "_type" property actual name.
338   private final Map<BeanPropertyMeta,BeanRegistryLookup> propertyBeanRegistries;  // Per-property bean-registry side-map that keeps registry implementation details off BeanPropertyMeta itself. Typed against the commons.bean SPI seam.
339
340   /**
341    * Creates a {@link BeanMeta} for the specified class using the supplied {@link BeanConfigContext}.
342    *
343    * <p>
344    * This is the bean-modeling entry point — it constructs a {@link BeanMeta} purely from a {@link Class}
345    * and a {@link BeanConfigContext}, without touching any marshalling-side type infrastructure.
346    * The returned {@link BeanMeta} carries enough information to do raw getter/setter invocation and property
347    * iteration; marshalling-aware reads ({@link #getBeanInfo()}, type-resolution on each
348    * {@link BeanPropertyMeta#getBeanInfo()}, the per-property registry lookup) remain <jk>null</jk>.
349    *
350    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
351    * <p class='bjava'>
352    *    BeanMeta&lt;Person&gt; <jv>bm</jv> = BeanMeta.<jsm>of</jsm>(Person.<jk>class</jk>, BeanConfigContext.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>);
353    *    BeanPropertyMeta <jv>name</jv> = <jv>bm</jv>.getProperties().get(<js>"name"</js>);
354    * </p>
355    *
356    * @param <T> The bean class type.
357    * @param beanClass The class to build a {@link BeanMeta} for.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
358    * @param config The bean-modeling configuration to apply.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
359    * @return A new {@link BeanMeta} for the specified class.
360    */
361   public static <T> BeanMeta<T> of(Class<T> beanClass, BeanConfigContext config) {
362      return new BeanMeta<>(beanClass, config);
363   }
364
365   /**
366    * Same as {@link #of(Class, BeanConfigContext)} using {@link BeanConfigContext#DEFAULT}.
367    *
368    * @param <T> The bean class type.
369    * @param beanClass The class to build a {@link BeanMeta} for.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
370    * @return A new {@link BeanMeta} for the specified class.
371    */
372   public static <T> BeanMeta<T> of(Class<T> beanClass) {
373      return of(beanClass, BeanConfigContext.DEFAULT);
374   }
375
376   /**
377    * Constructor.
378    *
379    * <p>
380    * Creates a new {@link BeanMeta} instance for the specified class metadata. This constructor performs
381    * introspection to discover all bean properties, methods, and fields in the class hierarchy.
382    *
383    * <p>
384    * The bean metadata is built by:
385    * <ul>
386    *    <li>Finding all bean fields in the class hierarchy
387    *    <li>Finding all bean methods (getters, setters, extraKeys) in the class hierarchy
388    *    <li>Determining the appropriate constructor for bean instantiation
389    *    <li>Building the class hierarchy for property discovery
390    *    <li>Creating the bean registry for dictionary name resolution
391    *    <li>Validating and filtering properties based on bean filter settings
392    * </ul>
393    *
394    * @param cm The class metadata for the bean class.
395    * @param bf Optional bean filter to apply. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
396    * @param pNames Explicit list of property names and order. If <jk>null</jk>, properties are determined automatically.
397    * @param implClass Optional implementation class constructor to use if one cannot be found. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
398    */
399   protected BeanMeta(BeanInfo<T> cm, BeanFilter bf, String[] pNames, ClassInfo implClass) {
400      this(cm, cm, cm.getBeanConfigContext(), cm.getMarshallingContext(), bf, pNames, implClass);
401   }
402
403   /**
404    * Bean-modeling constructor — builds a {@link BeanMeta} without marshalling context state.
405    *
406    * <p>
407    * See {@link #of(Class, BeanConfigContext)} for the public entry point.  The {@link #getBeanInfo()},
408    * {@link #getBeanTypeResolver() bean type resolver}, and per-property registry lookups are
409    * left <jk>null</jk>; the per-property {@link BeanPropertyMeta#getBeanInfo() rawTypeMeta}/{@code typeMeta}
410    * fields are also <jk>null</jk> (no type-resolution is performed in this path).
411    *
412    * @param beanClass The bean class.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
413    * @param config The bean-modeling configuration.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
414    */
415   protected BeanMeta(Class<T> beanClass, BeanConfigContext config) {
416      this(null, info(assertArgNotNull("beanClass", beanClass)), assertArgNotNull("config", config), null, null, null, null);
417   }
418
419   @SuppressWarnings({
420      "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for bean metadata initialization
421      "java:S107"   // 7 parameters needed to support both construction paths
422   })
423   private BeanMeta(BeanInfo<T> cm, ClassInfo ci0, BeanConfigContext config, Object mc, BeanFilter bf, String[] pNames, ClassInfo implClass) {
424      classMeta = cm;
425      classInfo = ci0;
426      this.config = config;
427      marshallingContext = mc;
428      beanFilter = bf;
429      implClassConstructor = opt(implClass).map(x -> x.getPublicConstructor(x2 -> x2.hasNumParameters(0)).orElse(null)).orElse(null);
430      fluentSetters = config.isFindFluentSetters() || (nn(bf) && bf.isFluentSetters());
431      stopClass = opt(bf).map(x -> x.getStopClass()).orElse(info(Object.class));
432      beanRegistry = memoize(this::findBeanRegistry);
433      classHierarchy = memoize(this::findClassHierarchy);
434      beanConstructor = findBeanConstructor();
435
436      // Local variables for initialization
437      var ap = config.getAnnotationProvider();
438      var c = classInfo.inner();
439      var ci = classInfo;
440      String notABeanReasonTemp = null;
441      var propertiesValue = Holder.<Map<String,BeanPropertyMeta>>empty();
442      var hiddenPropertiesMap = CollectionUtils.<String,BeanPropertyMeta>map();
443      var getterPropsMap = CollectionUtils.<Method,String>map();  // Convert to MethodInfo keys
444      var setterPropsMap = CollectionUtils.<Method,String>map();
445      var dynaPropertyValue = Holder.<BeanPropertyMeta>empty();
446      var propertyBeanRegistriesTemp = CollectionUtils.<BeanPropertyMeta,BeanRegistryLookup>map();  // Per-property BeanRegistry side-map.
447      var unsortedPropertiesTemp = false;
448      var btList = ap.find(org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.BeanType.class, classInfo);
449      var propertyNamer = opt(bf).map(x -> x.getPropertyNamer()).orElse(config.getPropertyNamer());
450
451      // resolveTypePropertyName may return null on the commons-side NOOP path; fall back to the configured default.
452      var resolvedTypePropertyName = config.getBeanMetaInitializer().resolveTypePropertyName(config, classInfo);
453      this.typePropertyName = nn(resolvedTypePropertyName) ? resolvedTypePropertyName : config.getBeanTypePropertyName();
454
455      // Check if constructor is required but not found (records are exempt since they use canonical constructors)
456      if (! beanConstructor.constructor().isPresent() && bf == null && config.isBeansRequireDefaultConstructor() && ! ci.isRecord())
457         notABeanReasonTemp = "Class does not have the required no-arg constructor";
458
459      var bfo = opt(bf);
460      var fixedBeanProps = bfo.map(x -> x.getProperties()).orElse(sete());
461
462      try {
463         Map<String,BeanPropertyMeta.Builder> normalProps = map();  // NOAI
464
465         // First populate the properties with those specified in the bean annotation to
466         // ensure that ordering first.
467         fixedBeanProps.forEach(x -> normalProps.put(x, BeanPropertyMeta.builder(this, x)));
468
469         if (config.isUseJavaBeanIntrospector()) {
470            var c2 = bfo.map(x -> x.getInterfaceClass()).filter(Objects::nonNull).orElse(classInfo);
471            java.beans.BeanInfo bi = null;
472            if (! c2.isInterface())
473               bi = Introspector.getBeanInfo(c2.inner(), stopClass.inner());
474            else
475               bi = Introspector.getBeanInfo(c2.inner(), null);
476            if (nn(bi))
477               mergeJavaBeanPropertyDescriptorsIntoNormalProps(bi, normalProps, propertyNamer);
478
479         } else /* Use 'better' introspection */ {
480
481            findBeanFields().forEach(x -> {
482               var name = ap.find(x).stream()
483                  .filter(x2 -> x2.isType(BeanProp.class) || x2.isType(Name.class))
484                  .map(BeanMeta::name)
485                  .filter(Objects::nonNull)
486                  .findFirst()
487                  .orElse(propertyNamer.getPropertyName(x.getName()));
488               name = resolveBeanFieldPropertyName(x, name, propertyNamer);
489               if (nn(name)) {
490                  normalProps.computeIfAbsent(name, n->BeanPropertyMeta.builder(this, n)).setField(x);
491               }
492            });
493
494            var bms = findBeanMethods();
495
496            // Iterate through all the getters.
497            bms.forEach(x -> {
498               var pn = x.propertyName;
499               var m = x.method;  // Method is read first for name/annotation precedence checks, then wrapped as MethodInfo.
500               var mi = info(m);
501               var bpm = normalProps.computeIfAbsent(pn, k -> new BeanPropertyMeta.Builder(this, k));
502
503               if (x.methodType == GETTER) {
504                  // Two getters.  Pick the best.
505                  if (nn(bpm.getter)
506                     && ((! ap.has(BeanProp.class, mi) && ap.has(BeanProp.class, bpm.getter))
507                        || (m.getName().startsWith("is") && bpm.getter.getNameSimple().startsWith("get")))) {
508                     // @BeanProp on existing getter takes precedence; else getX() overrides isX().
509                     m = bpm.getter.inner();
510                  }
511                  bpm.setGetter(info(m));
512               }
513            });
514
515            // Now iterate through all the setters.
516            bms.stream().filter(x -> eq(x.methodType, SETTER)).forEach(x -> {
517               var bpm = normalProps.get(x.propertyName);
518               if (x.matchesPropertyType(bpm))
519                  bpm.setSetter(info(x.method));
520            });
521
522            // Now iterate through all the extraKeys.
523            bms.stream().filter(x -> eq(x.methodType, EXTRAKEYS)).forEach(x -> normalProps.get(x.propertyName).setExtraKeys(info(x.method)));
524         }
525
526         var typeVarImpls = TypeVariables.of(c);
527
528         // Eliminate invalid properties, and set the contents of getterProps and setterProps.
529         var readOnlyProps = bfo.map(x -> x.getReadOnlyProperties()).orElse(sete());
530         var writeOnlyProps = bfo.map(x -> x.getWriteOnlyProperties()).orElse(sete());
531         for (var i = normalProps.values().iterator(); i.hasNext();) {
532            var p = i.next();
533            validateAndRegisterProperty(p, c, typeVarImpls, readOnlyProps, writeOnlyProps, i, getterPropsMap, setterPropsMap);
534         }
535
536         // Check for missing properties.
537         fixedBeanProps.stream().filter(x -> ! normalProps.containsKey(x)).findFirst().ifPresent(x -> { throw bex(c, "The property ''{0}'' was defined on the @BeanType(properties=X) annotation of class ''{1}'' but was not found on the class definition.", x, ci.getNameSimple()); });
538
539         // For records with renamed properties, remap constructor args to use the actual property names.
540         if (ci.isRecord() && ne(beanConstructor.args())) {
541            var components = ci.getRecordComponents();
542            var remappedArgs = new ArrayList<String>(beanConstructor.args().size());
543            for (int idx = 0; idx < beanConstructor.args().size(); idx++) {
544               var componentName = beanConstructor.args().get(idx);
545               if (normalProps.containsKey(componentName)) {
546                  remappedArgs.add(componentName);
547               } else {
548                  var rcName = idx < components.size() ? components.get(idx).getName() : componentName;
549                  var found = normalProps.entrySet().stream()
550                     .filter(e -> (e.getValue().field != null && e.getValue().field.hasName(rcName))
551                        || (e.getValue().getter != null && e.getValue().getter.hasName(rcName)))
552                     .map(Map.Entry::getKey)
553                     .findFirst()
554                     .orElse(componentName);
555                  remappedArgs.add(found);
556               }
557            }
558            beanConstructor = new BeanConstructor(beanConstructor.constructor(), remappedArgs);
559         }
560
561         // Mark constructor arg properties.
562         for (var fp : beanConstructor.args()) {
563            var m = normalProps.get(fp);
564            if (m == null)
565               throw bex(c, "The property ''{0}'' was defined on the @BeanCtor(properties=X) annotation but was not found on the class definition.", fp);
566            m.setAsConstructorArg();
567         }
568
569         // Make sure at least one property was found (records with no components are exempt).
570         if (bf == null && config.isBeansRequireSomeProperties() && normalProps.isEmpty() && ! ci.isRecord())
571            notABeanReasonTemp = "No properties detected on bean class";
572
573         unsortedPropertiesTemp = config.isUnsortedProperties() || bfo.map(x -> x.isUnsortedProperties()).orElse(false) || !fixedBeanProps.isEmpty();
574
575         propertiesValue.set(unsortedPropertiesTemp ? map() : sortedMap());
576
577      normalProps.forEach((k, v) -> {
578         var pMeta = v.build();
579         if (pMeta.isDyna())
580            dynaPropertyValue.set(pMeta);
581         propertiesValue.get().put(k, pMeta);
582         // Build the property-level BeanRegistry side-map entry from the builder's accumulated
583         // dictionary classes.  Parents to the bean-level registry so @MarshalledProp(dictionary={})
584         // entries chain on top of bean and global dictionaries.  Skipped on the commons-side path
585         // (no marshallingContext means no BeanRegistry construction).
586         if (nn(marshallingContext) && nn(v.dictionaryClasses))
587            propertyBeanRegistriesTemp.put(pMeta, config.getBeanMetaInitializer().buildPropertyBeanRegistry(marshallingContext, beanRegistry.get(), v.dictionaryClasses));
588      });
589
590         // If a beanFilter is defined, look for inclusion and exclusion lists.
591         if (bf != null) {
592
593            // Eliminated excluded properties if MarshalledFilter.excludeKeys is specified.
594            var bfbpi = bf.getProperties();
595            var bfbpx = bf.getExcludeProperties();
596            var p = propertiesValue.get();
597
598            if (! bfbpi.isEmpty()) {
599               // Only include specified properties if MarshalledFilter.includeKeys is specified.
600               // Note that the order must match includeKeys.
601               Map<String,BeanPropertyMeta> p2 = map();  // NOAI
602               bfbpi.stream().filter(p::containsKey).forEach(x -> p2.put(x, p.remove(x)));
603               hiddenPropertiesMap.putAll(p);
604               propertiesValue.set(p2);
605            }
606
607            bfbpx.forEach(x -> hiddenPropertiesMap.put(x, propertiesValue.get().remove(x)));
608         }
609
610         if (nn(pNames)) {
611            var p = propertiesValue.get();
612            Map<String,BeanPropertyMeta> p2 = map();
613            for (var k : pNames) {
614               if (p.containsKey(k))
615                  p2.put(k, p.get(k));
616               else
617                  hiddenPropertiesMap.put(k, p.get(k));
618            }
619            propertiesValue.set(p2);
620         }
621
622      } catch (BeanRuntimeException e) {
623         throw e;
624      } catch (Exception e) {
625         notABeanReasonTemp = "Exception:  " + getStackTrace(e);
626      }
627
628      notABeanReason = notABeanReasonTemp;
629      properties = u(propertiesValue.get());
630      hiddenProperties = u(hiddenPropertiesMap);
631      getterProps = u(getterPropsMap);
632      setterProps = u(setterPropsMap);
633      dynaProperty = dynaPropertyValue.get();
634      unsortedProperties = unsortedPropertiesTemp;
635      typeProperty = BeanPropertyMeta.builder(this, typePropertyName).canRead().canWrite().rawMetaType(String.class).build();
636      // Map the synthetic "_type" property to the bean-level BeanRegistry so consumers calling
637      // typeProperty.getBeanRegistry() (currently none in-tree, but a public API path) get the same
638      // registry the property previously carried as a field.  Skipped on the commons-side path.
639      if (nn(marshallingContext))
640         propertyBeanRegistriesTemp.put(typeProperty, beanRegistry.get());
641      propertyBeanRegistries = u(propertyBeanRegistriesTemp);
642      dictionaryName = memoize(this::findDictionaryName);
643      beanProxyInvocationHandler = memoize(() -> config.isUseInterfaceProxies() && classInfo.isInterface() ? new BeanProxyInvocationHandler<>(this) : null);
644      var factoryClassTemp = btList.stream().map(x -> x.inner().factory()).filter(x -> x != org.apache.juneau.commons.function.BeanFactory.Void.class).findFirst().orElse(null);
645      factoryClass = factoryClassTemp;
646   }
647
648   @SuppressWarnings({
649      "java:S107" // 8 parameters needed for property validation context
650   })
651   private void validateAndRegisterProperty(BeanPropertyMeta.Builder p, Class<?> c, TypeVariables typeVarImpls, Set<String> readOnlyProps, Set<String> writeOnlyProps, Iterator<BeanPropertyMeta.Builder> i, Map<Method,String> getterProps, Map<Method,String> setterProps) {
652      try {
653         if (p.field == null)
654            findInnerBeanField(p.name).ifPresent(p::setInnerField);
655
656         // When constructed via the commons-side path, marshallingContext is null and validate() runs in
657         // raw-reflection mode (no ClassMeta/ObjectSwap/BeanRegistry resolution).
658         if (p.validate((BeanTypeResolver) marshallingContext, typeVarImpls, readOnlyProps, writeOnlyProps)) {
659
660            // Marshalling-side post-processor — applies @MarshalledProp / @Swap annotation effects
661            // (swap detection, properties override, dictionary classes) and installs swap-aware
662            // read/write transforms.  Skipped on the commons-side path: those annotations require a
663            // MarshallingContext to resolve ObjectSwap/StringFormatSwap/Surrogate types and the swap
664            // class meta.
665            if (nn(marshallingContext))
666               config.getBeanPropertyPostProcessor().process(marshallingContext, p);
667
668            if (nn(p.getter))
669               getterProps.put(p.getter.inner(), p.name);
670
671            if (nn(p.setter))
672               setterProps.put(p.setter.inner(), p.name);
673
674         } else {
675            i.remove();
676         }
677      } catch (Exception e) {
678         throw bex(c, lm(e));
679      }
680   }
681
682   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
683   public boolean equals(Object o) {
684      return (o instanceof BeanMeta<?> o2) && eq(this, o2, (x, y) -> eq(x.classInfo, y.classInfo));
685   }
686
687   /**
688    * Returns the bean filter associated with this bean.
689    *
690    * <p>
691    * Bean filters are used to control aspects of how beans are handled during serialization and parsing, such as
692    * property inclusion/exclusion, property ordering, and type name mapping.
693    *
694    * <p>
695    * On the marshalling-side construction path, the bean filter is typically built from the {@code @Marshalled} and
696    * {@link BeanType @BeanType} annotations on the class.  If neither annotation is present (or this {@link BeanMeta}
697    * was built via the commons-side path), this method returns <jk>null</jk>.
698    *
699    * @return The bean filter for this bean, or <jk>null</jk> if no bean filter is associated with this bean.
700    */
701   public BeanFilter getBeanFilter() {
702      // The field is typed as the bean-modeling-side BeanFilter SPI; marshalling-side callers
703      // narrow back to MarshalledFilter (the only concrete implementation in-tree).
704      return beanFilter;
705   }
706
707   /**
708    * Returns the proxy invocation handler for this bean if it's an interface.
709    *
710    * @return The invocation handler, or <jk>null</jk> if this is not an interface or interface proxies are disabled.
711    */
712   public InvocationHandler getBeanProxyInvocationHandler() {
713      return beanProxyInvocationHandler.get();
714   }
715
716   /**
717    * Returns the bean registry for this bean.
718    *
719    * <p>
720    * The bean registry is used to resolve dictionary names to class types. It's created when a bean class has a
721    * {@code @Marshalled(dictionary)} annotation that specifies a list of possible subclasses.
722    *
723    * <p>
724    * Returns the bean-modeling-side SPI type ({@link BeanRegistryLookup}).
725    *
726    * @return The bean registry for this bean, or <jk>null</jk> if no bean registry is associated with it.
727    */
728   public BeanRegistryLookup getBeanRegistry() { return beanRegistry.get(); }
729
730   /**
731    * Returns the per-property registry lookup associated with the given {@link BeanPropertyMeta}.
732    *
733    * <p>
734    * {@link BeanPropertyMeta} no longer carries a concrete registry field — the per-property
735    * registry now lives in a side-map on this {@link BeanMeta} keyed by the property meta itself.  The serializer
736    * and parser sides still need to look up the property-level registry for polymorphic dispatch; they now route through
737    * this accessor (directly or via the deprecated-style {@link BeanPropertyMeta#getBeanRegistry()} delegate).
738    *
739    * <p>
740    * The returned registry chains the property's
741    * {@code @MarshalledProp(dictionary)} entries on top of
742    * the bean-level registry, which in turn chains on top of the global bean dictionary.
743    *
744    * @param p The bean property meta to look up.  Can be a normal property, the synthetic <js>"_type"</js> property,
745    *    or any other property meta produced by this bean.
746    * @return The bean registry for the specified property, or <jk>null</jk> if none was registered (e.g. the property
747    *    belongs to a different bean meta).
748    */
749   public BeanRegistryLookup getPropertyBeanRegistry(BeanPropertyMeta p) {
750      return propertyBeanRegistries.get(p);
751   }
752
753   /**
754    * Returns the {@link BeanInfo} of this bean.
755    *
756    * <p>
757    * Returns <jk>null</jk> when this {@link BeanMeta} was constructed via the commons-side path
758    * ({@link #of(Class, BeanConfigContext)}) — in that case {@link #getClassInfo()} carries the
759    * pure-reflection view of the bean class.
760    *
761    * <p>
762    * Returns the bean-modeling-side SPI type ({@link BeanInfo}).
763    *
764    * @return The {@link BeanInfo} of this bean, or <jk>null</jk> for bean-modeling-only construction.
765    */
766   public BeanInfo<T> getBeanInfo() { return classMeta; }
767
768   /**
769    * Returns the {@link ClassInfo} of this bean.
770    *
771    * <p>
772    * Pure-reflection view of the bean class.  Always non-null, regardless of construction path.  Use this in
773    * preference to {@link #getBeanInfo()} for any bean-modeling read that does not depend on marshalling-aware
774    * type metadata; the commons-side construction path leaves {@link #getBeanInfo()} <jk>null</jk>.
775    *
776    * @return The class info for this bean.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
777    */
778   public ClassInfo getClassInfo() { return classInfo; }
779
780   /**
781    * Returns the bean-modeling configuration snapshot used to build this {@link BeanMeta}.
782    *
783    * <p>
784    * For marshalling-side construction, this is sourced from the owning marshalling context.
785    * For commons-side construction (via {@link #of(Class, BeanConfigContext)}), this is the {@link BeanConfigContext}
786    * passed to the factory.  Always non-null.
787    *
788    * @return The bean-modeling configuration.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
789    */
790   public BeanConfigContext getConfig() { return config; }
791
792   /**
793    * Returns the dictionary name for this bean as defined through the {@code @Marshalled(typeName)} annotation.
794    *
795    * @return The dictionary name for this bean, or <jk>null</jk> if it has no dictionary name defined.
796    */
797   public String getDictionaryName() { return dictionaryName.get(); }
798
799   /**
800    * Returns a map of all properties on this bean.
801    *
802    * <p>
803    * The map is keyed by property name and contains {@link BeanPropertyMeta} objects that provide metadata about each
804    * property, including its type, getter/setter methods, field information, and serialization/parsing behavior.
805    *
806    * <p>
807    * This map contains only the normal (non-hidden) properties of the bean. Hidden properties can be accessed via
808    * {@link #getHiddenProperties()}.
809    *
810    * @return A map of property names to their metadata. The map is unmodifiable.
811    * @see #getPropertyMeta(String)
812    * @see #getHiddenProperties()
813    */
814   public Map<String,BeanPropertyMeta> getProperties() { return properties; }
815
816   /**
817    * Returns metadata about the specified property.
818    *
819    * @param name The name of the property on this bean.
820    * @return The metadata about the property, or <jk>null</jk> if no such property exists on this bean.
821    */
822   public BeanPropertyMeta getPropertyMeta(String name) {
823      if (name == null)
824         return dynaProperty;
825      var bpm = properties.get(name);
826      if (bpm == null)
827         bpm = hiddenProperties.get(name);
828      if (bpm == null)
829         bpm = dynaProperty;
830      return bpm;
831   }
832
833   /**
834    * Returns a mock bean property that resolves to the name <js>"_type"</js> and whose value always resolves to the
835    * dictionary name of the bean.
836    *
837    * @return The type name property.
838    */
839   public BeanPropertyMeta getTypeProperty() { return typeProperty; }
840
841   /**
842    * Returns the type property name for this bean.
843    *
844    * <p>
845    * This is the name of the bean property used to store the dictionary name of a bean type so that the parser knows
846    * the data type to reconstruct.
847    *
848    * <p>
849    * If <jk>null</jk>, <js>"_type"</js> should be assumed.
850    *
851    * <p>
852    * The value is determined from:
853    * <ul>
854    *    <li>The marshalling-side {@code @Marshalled(typePropertyName)} on the class, if present
855    *       (resolved via {@link BeanMetaInitializer#resolveTypePropertyName(BeanConfigContext, ClassInfo)}).
856    *    <li>Otherwise, the default from {@link BeanConfigContext#getBeanTypePropertyName()}.
857    * </ul>
858    *
859    * @return
860    *    The type property name associated with this bean, or <jk>null</jk> if the default <js>"_type"</js> should be used.
861    * @see BeanConfigContext#getBeanTypePropertyName()
862    */
863   public String getTypePropertyName() { return typePropertyName; }
864
865   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
866   public int hashCode() {
867      return classInfo.hashCode();
868   }
869
870   /**
871    * Property read interceptor.
872    *
873    * <p>
874    * Called immediately after calling the getter to allow the value to be overridden.
875    *
876    * @param bean The bean from which the property was read.
877    * @param name The property name.
878    * @param value The value just extracted from calling the bean getter.
879    * @return The value to serialize.  Default is just to return the existing value.
880    */
881   public Object onReadProperty(Object bean, String name, Object value) {
882      return beanFilter == null ? value : beanFilter.readProperty(bean, name, value);
883   }
884
885   /**
886    * Property write interceptor.
887    *
888    * <p>
889    * Called immediately before calling theh setter to allow value to be overwridden.
890    *
891    * @param bean The bean from which the property was read.
892    * @param name The property name.
893    * @param value The value just parsed.
894    * @return The value to serialize.  Default is just to return the existing value.
895    */
896   public Object onWriteProperty(Object bean, String name, Object value) {
897      return beanFilter == null ? value : beanFilter.writeProperty(bean, name, value);
898   }
899
900   protected FluentMap<String,Object> properties() {
901      // @formatter:off
902      return filteredBeanPropertyMap()
903         .a(PROP_class, classInfo.getName())
904         .a(PROP_properties, properties);
905      // @formatter:on
906   }
907
908   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
909   public String toString() {
910      return r(properties());
911   }
912
913   /**
914    * Returns the bean-modeling {@link BeanTypeResolver} that this metadata was constructed against.
915    *
916    * <p>
917    * On the marshalling-side construction path, the underlying object is the marshalling context (which
918    * implements {@link BeanTypeResolver}); marshalling-side callers that need the concrete narrowing must cast
919    * (e.g. {@code (MarshallingContext) bm.getBeanTypeResolver()}).
920    *
921    * <p>
922    * Returns <jk>null</jk> when this {@link BeanMeta} was constructed via the commons-side path
923    * ({@link #of(Class, BeanConfigContext)}).  Use {@link #getConfig()} for the always-non-null bean-modeling
924    * configuration facade.
925    *
926    * @return The bean-modeling type resolver, or <jk>null</jk> for bean-modeling-only construction.
927    */
928   protected BeanTypeResolver getBeanTypeResolver() { return (BeanTypeResolver) marshallingContext; }
929
930   /**
931    * Returns the constructor for this bean, if one was found.
932    *
933    * <p>
934    * The constructor is determined by {@link #findBeanConstructor()} and may be:
935    * <ul>
936    *    <li>A constructor annotated with {@link BeanCtor @BeanCtor}
937    *    <li>An implementation class constructor (if provided)
938    *    <li>A no-argument constructor
939    *    <li><jk>null</jk> if no suitable constructor was found
940    * </ul>
941    *
942    * @return The constructor for this bean, or <jk>null</jk> if no constructor is available.
943    * @see #getConstructorArgs()
944    * @see #hasConstructor()
945    */
946   public ConstructorInfo getConstructor() {
947      return beanConstructor.constructor().orElse(null);
948   }
949
950   /**
951    * Returns the list of property names that correspond to the constructor parameters.
952    *
953    * <p>
954    * The property names are in the same order as the constructor parameters. These names are used to map
955    * parsed property values to constructor arguments when creating bean instances.
956    *
957    * <p>
958    * The property names are determined from:
959    * <ul>
960    *    <li>The {@link BeanCtor#properties() properties()} value in the {@link BeanCtor @BeanCtor} annotation (if present)
961    *    <li>Otherwise, the parameter names from the constructor (if available in bytecode)
962    * </ul>
963    *
964    * <p>
965    * If the bean has no constructor or uses a no-argument constructor, this list will be empty.
966    *
967    * @return A list of property names corresponding to constructor parameters, in parameter order.
968    * @see #getConstructor()
969    * @see #hasConstructor()
970    */
971   public List<String> getConstructorArgs() {
972      return beanConstructor.args();
973   }
974
975   /**
976    * Returns the "extras" property for dynamic bean properties.
977    *
978    * @return The dynamic property, or <jk>null</jk> if not present.
979    */
980   protected BeanPropertyMeta getDynaProperty() { return dynaProperty; }
981
982   /**
983    * Returns the map of getter methods to property names.
984    *
985    * @return The getter properties map.
986    */
987   protected Map<Method,String> getGetterProps() { return getterProps; }
988
989   /**
990    * Returns the map of hidden properties on this bean.
991    *
992    * <p>
993    * Hidden properties are properties that exist on the bean but are not included in the normal property list.
994    * These properties are typically excluded from serialization but may still be accessible programmatically.
995    *
996    * <p>
997    * Hidden properties can be defined through:
998    * <ul>
999    *    <li>{@link BeanFilter#getExcludeProperties() Bean filter exclude properties}
1000    *    <li>Properties that fail validation during bean metadata creation
1001    * </ul>
1002    *
1003    * @return A map of hidden property names to their metadata. The map is unmodifiable.
1004    * @see #getProperties()
1005    */
1006   protected Map<String,BeanPropertyMeta> getHiddenProperties() {
1007      return hiddenProperties;
1008   }
1009
1010   /**
1011    * Returns the map of setter methods to property names.
1012    *
1013    * @return The setter properties map.
1014    */
1015   protected Map<Method,String> getSetterProps() { return setterProps; }
1016
1017   /**
1018    * Returns whether this bean has a constructor available for instantiation.
1019    *
1020    * <p>
1021    * A bean has a constructor if {@link #findBeanConstructor()} was able to find a suitable constructor,
1022    * which may be:
1023    * <ul>
1024    *    <li>A constructor annotated with {@link BeanCtor @BeanCtor}
1025    *    <li>An implementation class constructor (if provided)
1026    *    <li>A no-argument constructor
1027    * </ul>
1028    *
1029    * <p>
1030    * If this method returns <jk>false</jk>, the bean cannot be instantiated using {@link #newBean(Object)},
1031    * and may need to be created through other means (e.g., interface proxies).
1032    *
1033    * @return <jk>true</jk> if a constructor is available, <jk>false</jk> otherwise.
1034    * @see #getConstructor()
1035    * @see #newBean(Object)
1036    */
1037   public boolean hasConstructor() {
1038      return beanConstructor.constructor().isPresent();
1039   }
1040
1041   /**
1042    * Returns whether this bean has opted out of alphabetical property sorting.
1043    *
1044    * @return <jk>true</jk> if properties should use natural JVM-dependent order.
1045    */
1046   protected boolean isUnsortedProperties() { return unsortedProperties; }
1047
1048   /**
1049    * Creates a new instance of this bean.
1050    *
1051    * @param outer The outer object if bean class is a non-static inner member class.
1052    * @return A new instance of this bean if possible, or <jk>null</jk> if not.
1053    * @throws ExecutableException Exception occurred on invoked constructor/method/field.
1054    */
1055   @SuppressWarnings({
1056      "unchecked", // Type erasure requires unchecked cast
1057      "rawtypes" // Raw BeanFactory type used at runtime for factory resolution
1058   })
1059   public T newBean(Object outer) throws ExecutableException {
1060      if (factoryClass != null) {
1061         try {
1062            BeanFactory factory = resolveFactory(factoryClass);
1063            return (T) factory.create();
1064         } catch (ExecutableException e) {
1065            throw e;
1066         } catch (Exception e) {
1067            throw new ExecutableException(e);
1068         }
1069      }
1070      if (classInfo.isMemberClass() && classInfo.isNotStatic()) {
1071         if (hasConstructor())
1072            return getConstructor().<T>newInstance(outer);
1073      } else {
1074         if (hasConstructor())
1075            return getConstructor().<T>newInstance();
1076         var h = beanProxyInvocationHandler.get();
1077         if (nn(h)) {
1078            var inner = classInfo.inner();
1079            return (T)Proxy.newProxyInstance(inner.getClassLoader(), a(inner, java.io.Serializable.class), h);
1080         }
1081      }
1082      return null;
1083   }
1084
1085   @SuppressWarnings({
1086      "rawtypes", // Raw BeanFactory type at runtime
1087      "unchecked" // Unchecked casts required for factory class and BeanStore result
1088   })
1089   private org.apache.juneau.commons.function.BeanFactory resolveFactory(Class<? extends org.apache.juneau.commons.function.BeanFactory> fc) {
1090      var bs = config.getBeanStore();
1091      if (bs != null) {
1092         var opt = bs.getBean(fc);
1093         if (opt.isPresent())
1094            return opt.get();
1095      }
1096      return (BeanFactory) BeanInstantiator.of((Class)fc).run();
1097   }
1098
1099   /*
1100    * Finds the appropriate constructor for this bean and determines the property names for constructor arguments.
1101    *
1102    * <p>
1103    * This method searches for a constructor in the following order of precedence:
1104    * <ol>
1105    *    <li><b>{@link BeanCtor @BeanCtor} annotated constructor:</b> If a constructor is annotated with {@link BeanCtor @BeanCtor},
1106    *       it is used. The property names are determined from:
1107    *       <ul>
1108    *          <li>The {@link BeanCtor#properties() properties()} value in the annotation, if specified
1109    *          <li>Otherwise, the parameter names from the constructor (if available in bytecode)
1110    *       </ul>
1111    *       If multiple constructors are annotated with {@link BeanCtor @BeanCtor}, an exception is thrown.
1112    *    <li><b>Implementation class constructor:</b> If an {@link #implClassConstructor} was provided during bean
1113    *       metadata creation, it is used with an empty property list.
1114    *    <li><b>No-arg constructor:</b> Searches for a no-argument constructor. The visibility required depends on
1115    *       whether the class carries a bean-registration annotation (e.g. {@link BeanType @BeanType} or the
1116    *       marshalling-side {@code @Marshalled}) — see
1117    *       {@link BeanMetaInitializer#hasBeanRegistrationAnnotation(BeanConfigContext, ClassInfo)}:
1118    *       <ul>
1119    *          <li>If a registration annotation is present, private constructors are allowed
1120    *          <li>Otherwise, the visibility is determined by {@link BeanConfigContext#getBeanConstructorVisibility()}
1121    *       </ul>
1122    *    <li><b>No constructor:</b> Returns an empty {@link Optional} if no suitable constructor is found.
1123    * </ol>
1124    *
1125    * <p>
1126    * The returned {@link BeanConstructor} contains:
1127    * <ul>
1128    *    <li>The constructor (if found), wrapped in an {@link Optional}
1129    *    <li>A list of property names that correspond to the constructor parameters, in order
1130    * </ul>
1131    *
1132    * @return A {@link BeanConstructor} containing the found constructor and its associated property names.
1133    * @throws BeanRuntimeException If multiple constructors are annotated with {@link BeanCtor @BeanCtor}, or if
1134    *    the number of properties specified in {@link BeanCtor @BeanCtor} doesn't match the number of constructor parameters,
1135    *    or if parameter names cannot be determined from the bytecode.
1136    */
1137   @SuppressWarnings({
1138      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for constructor finding logic
1139   })
1140   private BeanConstructor findBeanConstructor() {
1141      var ap = config.getAnnotationProvider();
1142      var vis = config.getBeanConstructorVisibility();
1143      var ci = classInfo;
1144
1145      var l = ci.getPublicConstructors().stream().filter(x -> ap.has(BeanCtor.class, x)).toList();
1146      if (l.isEmpty())
1147         l = ci.getDeclaredConstructors().stream().filter(x -> ap.has(BeanCtor.class, x)).toList();
1148      if (l.size() > 1)
1149         throw bex(ci, "Multiple instances of '@BeanCtor' found.");
1150      if (l.size() == 1) {
1151         var con = first(l).orElseThrow(() -> bex(ci, "No constructor found.")).accessible();
1152         var args = ap.find(BeanCtor.class, con).stream().map(x -> x.inner().properties()).filter(StringUtils::isNotBlank).map(x -> split(x)).findFirst().orElse(liste());
1153         if (! con.hasNumParameters(args.size())) {
1154            if (ne(args))
1155               throw bex(ci, "Number of properties defined in '@BeanCtor' annotation does not match number of parameters in constructor.");
1156            args = con.getParameters().stream().map(x -> x.getName()).toList();
1157            for (int i = 0; i < args.size(); i++) {
1158               if (isBlank(args.get(i)))
1159                  throw bex(ci, "Could not find name for parameter #{0} of constructor ''{1}''", i, con.getNameFull());
1160            }
1161         }
1162         return new BeanConstructor(opt(con), args);
1163      }
1164
1165      if (ci.isRecord()) {
1166         var components = ci.getRecordComponents();
1167         var paramTypes = components.stream().map(java.lang.reflect.RecordComponent::getType).toArray(Class[]::new);
1168         var rcon = ci.getPublicConstructor(x -> x.hasParameterTypes(paramTypes)).orElse(null);
1169         if (rcon != null)
1170            return new BeanConstructor(opt(rcon.accessible()), components.stream().map(java.lang.reflect.RecordComponent::getName).toList());
1171      }
1172
1173      if (implClassConstructor != null)
1174         return new BeanConstructor(opt(implClassConstructor.accessible()), liste());
1175
1176      var con = ci.getNoArgConstructor(config.getBeanMetaInitializer().hasBeanRegistrationAnnotation(config, classInfo) ? Visibility.PRIVATE : vis).orElse(null);
1177      if (con != null)
1178         return new BeanConstructor(opt(con.accessible()), liste());
1179
1180      return new BeanConstructor(opte(), liste());
1181   }
1182
1183   /**
1184    * Resolves the bean property name for a field when {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} or {@link Name @Name} supplies
1185    * <js>"*"</js>.
1186    *
1187    * <p>
1188    * On a {@link Map} field, <js>"*"</js> remains the dyna-property name. On any other field type, <js>"*"</js> is
1189    * treated like an unnamed {@code @BeanProp} (apply other attributes) but the property name is taken from the field
1190    * (via {@link PropertyNamer#getPropertyName(String)}).
1191    * </p>
1192    *
1193    * @param x Field being registered.
1194    * @param nameFromAnnotations Name from {@code @BeanProp}/{@code @Name}, or already-resolved default from the field name.
1195    * @param propertyNamer Namer for raw field names.
1196    * @return Property key to use in {@link BeanMeta}.
1197    */
1198   private static String resolveBeanFieldPropertyName(FieldInfo x, String nameFromAnnotations, PropertyNamer propertyNamer) {
1199      if (! "*".equals(nameFromAnnotations))
1200         return nameFromAnnotations;
1201      if (x.getFieldType().isAssignableTo(Map.class))
1202         return "*";
1203      return propertyNamer.getPropertyName(x.getName());
1204   }
1205
1206   /*
1207    * Finds all bean fields in the class hierarchy.
1208    *
1209    * <p>
1210    * Traverses the complete class hierarchy (as defined by {@link #classHierarchy}) and collects all fields that
1211    * meet the bean field criteria:
1212    * <ul>
1213    *    <li>Not static
1214    *    <li>Not transient (unless transient fields are not ignored)
1215    *    <li>Not annotated with {@link Transient @Transient} (unless transient fields are not ignored)
1216    *    <li>Not annotated with {@link BeanIgnore @BeanIgnore}
1217    *    <li>Visible according to the specified visibility level, or annotated with {@link BeanProp @BeanProp}
1218    * </ul>
1219    *
1220    * @return A collection of all bean fields found in the class hierarchy.
1221    */
1222   private Collection<FieldInfo> findBeanFields() {
1223      var v = config.getBeanFieldVisibility();
1224      var noIgnoreTransients = ! config.isIgnoreTransientFields();
1225      var ap = config.getAnnotationProvider();
1226      var isRecord = classInfo.isRecord();
1227      var recordComponentNames = isRecord
1228         ? classInfo.getRecordComponents().stream().map(java.lang.reflect.RecordComponent::getName).collect(java.util.stream.Collectors.toSet())
1229         : Set.<String>of();
1230      // @formatter:off
1231      return classHierarchy.get().stream()
1232         .flatMap(c2 -> c2.getDeclaredFields().stream())
1233         .filter(x -> x.isNotStatic()
1234            && (x.isNotTransient() || noIgnoreTransients)
1235            && (! x.hasAnnotation(Transient.class) || noIgnoreTransients)
1236            && ! ap.has(BeanIgnore.class, x)
1237            && (v.isVisible(x.inner()) || ap.has(BeanProp.class, x)
1238               || (isRecord && recordComponentNames.contains(x.getName()))))
1239         .toList();
1240      // @formatter:on
1241   }
1242
1243   /*
1244    * Finds all bean methods (getters, setters, and extraKeys) in the class hierarchy.
1245    *
1246    * <p>
1247    * Traverses the complete class hierarchy (as defined by {@link #classHierarchy}) and identifies methods that
1248    * represent bean properties. Methods are identified as:
1249    * <ul>
1250    *    <li><b>Getters:</b> Methods with no parameters that return a value, matching patterns like:
1251    *       <ul>
1252    *          <li><c>getX()</c> - standard getter pattern
1253    *          <li><c>isX()</c> - boolean getter pattern (returns boolean or Boolean)
1254    *          <li>Methods annotated with {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} or {@link Name @Name}
1255    *          <li>Methods with {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} annotation with value <js>"*"</js> that return a Map
1256    *       </ul>
1257    *    <li><b>Setters:</b> Methods with one parameter that match patterns like:
1258    *       <ul>
1259    *          <li><c>setX(value)</c> - standard setter pattern
1260    *          <li><c>withX(value)</c> - fluent setter pattern (returns the bean type)
1261    *          <li>Methods annotated with {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} or {@link Name @Name}
1262    *          <li>Methods with {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} annotation with value <js>"*"</js> that accept a Map
1263    *          <li>Fluent setters (if enabled) - methods that return the bean type and accept one parameter
1264    *       </ul>
1265    *    <li><b>ExtraKeys:</b> Methods with {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} annotation with value <js>"*"</js> that return a Collection
1266    * </ul>
1267    *
1268    * <p>
1269    * Methods are filtered based on:
1270    * <ul>
1271    *    <li>Not static, not bridge methods
1272    *    <li>Parameter count ≤ 2
1273    *    <li>Not annotated with {@link BeanIgnore @BeanIgnore}
1274    *    <li>Not annotated with {@link Transient @Transient}
1275    *    <li>Visible according to the specified visibility level, or annotated with {@link BeanProp @BeanProp} or {@link Name @Name}
1276    * </ul>
1277    *
1278    * <p>
1279    * Property names are determined from:
1280    * <ul>
1281    *    <li>{@link BeanProp @BeanProp} or {@link Name @Name} annotations (if present)
1282    *    <li>Otherwise, derived from the method name using the provided {@link PropertyNamer}
1283    * </ul>
1284    *
1285    * @return A list of {@link BeanMethod} objects representing all found bean methods.
1286    */
1287   @SuppressWarnings({
1288      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for bean method finding logic
1289   })
1290   private List<BeanMethod> findBeanMethods() {
1291      var l = new LinkedList<BeanMethod>();
1292      var ap = config.getAnnotationProvider();
1293      var ci = classInfo;
1294      var v = config.getBeanMethodVisibility();
1295      var pn = opt(beanFilter).map(x -> x.getPropertyNamer()).orElse(config.getPropertyNamer());
1296      var suppressedFromBeanIgnoredFields = findSuppressedPropertyNamesFromIgnoredFields(pn);
1297
1298      classHierarchy.get().forEach(c2 -> {
1299         for (var m : c2.getDeclaredMethods()) {
1300            var mm = m.getMatchingMethods();
1301            var beanps = ap.find(BeanProp.class, m).stream().map(AnnotationInfo::inner).toList();
1302            var names = ap.find(Name.class, m).stream().map(AnnotationInfo::inner).toList();
1303            // Skip static, bridge, or methods with >2 params; skip if ignored, transient, or not visible
1304            if (m.isStatic() || m.isBridge() || m.getParameterCount() > 2
1305               || mm.stream().anyMatch(m2 -> ap.has(BeanIgnore.class, m2, SELF))
1306               || mm.stream().anyMatch(m2 -> ap.find(Transient.class, m2, SELF).stream().map(x -> x.inner().value()).findFirst().orElse(false))
1307               || ! (m.isVisible(v) || ne(beanps) || ne(names)))
1308               continue;
1309
1310            var n = m.getNameSimple();
1311
1312            var params = m.getParameters();
1313            var rt = m.getReturnType();
1314            var methodType = UNKNOWN;
1315            var bpName = bpName(beanps, names);
1316
1317            if (params.isEmpty()) {
1318               if ("*".equals(bpName)) {
1319                  if (rt.isAssignableTo(Collection.class)) {
1320                     methodType = EXTRAKEYS;
1321                  } else if (rt.isAssignableTo(Map.class)) {
1322                     methodType = GETTER;
1323                  }
1324                  n = bpName;
1325               } else if (n.startsWith("get") && (! rt.is(Void.TYPE))) {
1326                  methodType = GETTER;
1327                  n = n.substring(3);
1328               } else if (n.startsWith("is") && (rt.is(Boolean.TYPE) || rt.is(Boolean.class))) {
1329                  methodType = GETTER;
1330                  n = n.substring(2);
1331               } else if (ci.isRecord() && isRecordAccessor(m, ci)) {
1332                  methodType = GETTER;
1333               } else if (nn(bpName)) {
1334                  methodType = GETTER;
1335                  if (bpName.isEmpty()) {
1336                     if (n.startsWith("get"))
1337                        n = n.substring(3);
1338                     else if (n.startsWith("is"))
1339                        n = n.substring(2);
1340                     bpName = n;
1341                  } else {
1342                     n = bpName;
1343                  }
1344               }
1345            } else if (params.size() == 1) {
1346               if ("*".equals(bpName)) {
1347                  if (params.get(0).getParameterType().isAssignableTo(Map.class)) {
1348                     methodType = SETTER;
1349                     n = bpName;
1350                  } else if (params.get(0).getParameterType().is(String.class)) {
1351                     methodType = GETTER;
1352                     n = bpName;
1353                  }
1354               } else if (n.startsWith("set") && (rt.isAssignableFrom(ci) || rt.is(Void.TYPE))) {
1355                  methodType = SETTER;
1356                  n = n.substring(3);
1357               } else if (n.startsWith("with") && (rt.isAssignableFrom(ci))) {
1358                  methodType = SETTER;
1359                  n = n.substring(4);
1360               } else if (nn(bpName)) {
1361                  methodType = SETTER;
1362                  if (bpName.isEmpty()) {
1363                     if (n.startsWith("set"))
1364                        n = n.substring(3);
1365                     bpName = n;
1366                  } else {
1367                     n = bpName;
1368                  }
1369               } else if (fluentSetters && rt.isAssignableFrom(ci)) {
1370                  methodType = SETTER;
1371               }
1372            } else if (params.size() == 2) {
1373               if ("*".equals(bpName) && params.get(0).getParameterType().is(String.class) && n.startsWith("set") && (rt.isAssignableFrom(ci) || rt.is(Void.TYPE))) {
1374                  methodType = SETTER;
1375               } else {
1376                  methodType = GETTER;
1377               }
1378               n = bpName;
1379            }
1380            n = pn.getPropertyName(n);
1381
1382            if ("*".equals(bpName) && methodType == UNKNOWN)
1383               throw bex(ci, "Found @BeanProp(\"*\") but could not determine method type on method ''{0}''.", m.getNameSimple());
1384
1385            if (methodType != UNKNOWN) {
1386               if (nn(bpName) && ! bpName.isEmpty())
1387                  n = bpName;
1388               if (nn(n) && ! suppressedFromBeanIgnoredFields.contains(n))
1389                  l.add(new BeanMethod(n, methodType, m.inner()));
1390            }
1391         }
1392      });
1393      return l;
1394   }
1395
1396   private static boolean isRecordAccessor(MethodInfo m, ClassInfo ci) {
1397      return ci.getRecordComponents().stream()
1398         .anyMatch(rc -> rc.getName().equals(m.getNameSimple())
1399            && rc.getType().equals(m.getReturnType().inner()));
1400   }
1401
1402   /*
1403    * Creates a bean registry for this bean class.
1404    *
1405    * <p>
1406    * The bean registry is used to resolve dictionary names (type names) to actual class types. This is essential
1407    * for polymorphic bean serialization and parsing, where a dictionary name in the serialized form needs to be
1408    * mapped back to the correct subclass.
1409    *
1410    * <p>
1411    * The registry is built from:
1412    * <ul>
1413    *    <li><b>Bean filter dictionary:</b> Classes specified in the {@link BeanFilter#getBeanDictionary() bean filter's dictionary}
1414    *       (if a bean filter is present)
1415    *    <li><b>{@code @Marshalled} annotation:</b> If the class has a {@code @Marshalled} annotation with a non-empty
1416    *       {@code typeName()}, the class itself is added to the dictionary
1417    * </ul>
1418    *
1419    * <p>
1420    * The registry is used by parsers to determine which class to instantiate when deserializing polymorphic beans.
1421    *
1422    * @return A new registry lookup containing the dictionary classes for this bean, or an empty lookup if
1423    *    no dictionary classes are found.
1424    */
1425   private BeanRegistryLookup findBeanRegistry() {
1426      // BeanRegistry is a marshalling-side concern (polymorphic dispatch via @Marshalled(typeName)/dictionary).
1427      // Lifted out to the BeanMetaInitializer SPI carried on BeanConfigContext so this class no longer references
1428      // MarshalledBeanMetaInitializer directly.  Returns null on the commons-side path (no marshallingContext).
1429      return config.getBeanMetaInitializer().buildBeanRegistry(marshallingContext, beanFilter, classInfo, config);
1430   }
1431
1432   /*
1433    * Builds a list of all classes in the class hierarchy for this bean.
1434    *
1435    * <p>
1436    * Traverses the complete inheritance hierarchy (classes and interfaces) starting from the bean class (or the
1437    * interface class specified in the bean filter) and collects all classes up to (but not including) the stop class.
1438    *
1439    * <p>
1440    * The traversal order follows a depth-first approach:
1441    * <ol>
1442    *    <li>First, recursively traverses the superclass hierarchy
1443    *    <li>Then, recursively traverses all implemented interfaces
1444    *    <li>Finally, adds the current class itself
1445    * </ol>
1446    *
1447    * <p>
1448    * If a {@link BeanFilter#getInterfaceClass() bean filter interface class} is specified, the traversal starts
1449    * from that interface class instead of the bean class itself. This allows beans to use properties defined on
1450    * a parent interface rather than the concrete implementation class.
1451    *
1452    * <p>
1453    * The resulting list is used to find bean properties, methods, and fields across the entire class hierarchy.
1454    *
1455    * @return An unmodifiable list of {@link ClassInfo} objects representing all classes in the hierarchy, in
1456    *    traversal order (superclasses first, then interfaces, then the class itself).
1457    */
1458   private List<ClassInfo> findClassHierarchy() {
1459      var result = new LinkedList<ClassInfo>();
1460      // If @BeanType(interfaceClass) is specified on the parent class, then we want
1461      // to use the properties defined on that class, not the subclass.
1462      var c2 = (nn(beanFilter) && nn(beanFilter.getInterfaceClass()) ? beanFilter.getInterfaceClass() : classInfo);
1463      findClassHierarchy(c2, stopClass, result::add);
1464      return u(result);
1465   }
1466
1467   /*
1468    * Recursively traverses the class hierarchy and invokes the consumer for each class found.
1469    *
1470    * <p>
1471    * This is a helper method that performs a depth-first traversal of the class hierarchy:
1472    * <ol>
1473    *    <li>Recursively processes the superclass (if present and not the stop class)
1474    *    <li>Recursively processes all implemented interfaces
1475    *    <li>Invokes the consumer with the current class
1476    * </ol>
1477    *
1478    * <p>
1479    * The traversal stops when it reaches the stop class (which is not included in the traversal).
1480    *
1481    * @param c The class to start traversal from.
1482    * @param stopClass The class to stop traversal at (exclusive). Traversal will not proceed beyond this class.
1483    * @param consumer The consumer to invoke for each class in the hierarchy.
1484    */
1485   private void findClassHierarchy(ClassInfo c, ClassInfo stopClass, Consumer<ClassInfo> consumer) {
1486      var sc = c.getSuperclass();
1487      if (nn(sc) && ! sc.is(stopClass.inner()))
1488         findClassHierarchy(sc, stopClass, consumer);
1489      c.getInterfaces().forEach(x -> findClassHierarchy(x, stopClass, consumer));
1490      consumer.accept(c);
1491   }
1492
1493   /*
1494    * Finds the dictionary name (type name) for this bean class.
1495    *
1496    * <p>
1497    * The dictionary name is used in serialized forms to identify the specific type of a bean instance, enabling
1498    * polymorphic serialization and deserialization. This is especially important when serializing/parsing beans
1499    * that are part of an inheritance hierarchy.
1500    *
1501    * <p>
1502    * The dictionary name is determined by searching in the following order of precedence:
1503    * <ol>
1504    *    <li><b>Bean filter type name:</b> If a bean filter is present and has a type name specified via
1505    *       {@link BeanFilter#getTypeName()}, that value is used.
1506    *    <li><b>Bean registry lookup:</b> If a bean registry exists for this bean, it is queried for the type name
1507    *       of this class.
1508    *    <li><b>Parent class registry lookup:</b> Searches through parent classes and interfaces (starting from the
1509    *       second one, skipping the class itself) and checks if any of their bean registries contain a type name
1510    *       for this class.
1511    *    <li><b>{@code @Marshalled} annotation:</b> If the class has a {@code @Marshalled} annotation with a non-empty
1512    *       {@code typeName()}, that value is used.
1513    *    <li><b>No dictionary name:</b> Returns <jk>null</jk> if no dictionary name is found.
1514    * </ol>
1515    *
1516    * <p>
1517    * If a dictionary name is found, it will be used in serialized output (typically as a special property like
1518    * <js>"_type"</js>) so that parsers can determine the correct class to instantiate when deserializing.
1519    *
1520    * @return The dictionary name for this bean, or <jk>null</jk> if no dictionary name is defined.
1521    */
1522   private String findDictionaryName() {
1523      if (nn(beanFilter) && nn(beanFilter.getTypeName()))
1524         return beanFilter.getTypeName();
1525
1526      // Pure-reflection class identity for BeanRegistry.getTypeName(...) — this method now uses
1527      // surviving `this.classMeta` references inside this method to `classInfo.inner()` so the dictionary
1528      // lookup does not require a ClassMeta for the bean's own class.  BeanRegistry still lives in
1529      // juneau-marshall; it just exposes a raw-Class overload for callers that have a ClassInfo.
1530      var rawClass = classInfo.inner();
1531
1532      var br = getBeanRegistry();
1533      if (nn(br)) {
1534         String s = br.getTypeName(rawClass);
1535         if (nn(s))
1536            return s;
1537      }
1538
1539      // Parent-class BeanRegistry lookup is a marshalling-side concern: it walks parents and interfaces to
1540      // see if any of THEIR ClassMeta-backed BeanRegistries declares a typeName for our raw class.  Lifted
1541      // out to the BeanMetaInitializer SPI carried on BeanConfigContext.  Returns null on the commons-side path.
1542      var bmi = config.getBeanMetaInitializer();
1543      var n = bmi.findTypeNameInParents(marshallingContext, classInfo, rawClass);
1544      if (n != null)
1545         return n;
1546
1547      return bmi.findMarshalledTypeName(config, classInfo);
1548   }
1549
1550   /*
1551    * Merges standard JavaBeans {@link BeanInfo} property descriptors into {@code normalProps}, skipping the class
1552    * pseudo-property and logical names suppressed when {@link BeanIgnore#ignoreAccessors()} is <jk>true</jk> on a field.
1553    */
1554   @SuppressWarnings({
1555      "java:S135" // Two continues: skip class pseudo-property and names suppressed via @BeanIgnore(ignoreAccessors)
1556   })
1557   private void mergeJavaBeanPropertyDescriptorsIntoNormalProps(java.beans.BeanInfo bi, Map<String,BeanPropertyMeta.Builder> normalProps,
1558         PropertyNamer propertyNamer) {
1559      var suppressedFromBeanIgnoredFields = findSuppressedPropertyNamesFromIgnoredFields(propertyNamer);
1560      for (var pd : bi.getPropertyDescriptors()) {
1561         if (PROP_class.equals(pd.getName()))
1562            continue;
1563         if (suppressedFromBeanIgnoredFields.contains(pd.getName()))
1564            continue;
1565         var builder = normalProps.computeIfAbsent(pd.getName(), n -> BeanPropertyMeta.builder(this, n));
1566         if (pd.getReadMethod() != null)
1567            builder.setGetter(info(pd.getReadMethod()));
1568         if (pd.getWriteMethod() != null)
1569            builder.setSetter(info(pd.getWriteMethod()));
1570      }
1571   }
1572
1573   /*
1574    * Property names suppressed from getter/setter discovery because a non-static field with that logical name is
1575    * annotated with {@link BeanIgnore @BeanIgnore} and {@link BeanIgnore#ignoreAccessors()} is <jk>true</jk>.
1576    *
1577    * <p>
1578    * When {@link BeanIgnore#ignoreAccessors()} is <jk>false</jk> (the default), ignored fields do not suppress
1579    * JavaBean accessors so patterns such as {@code @BeanIgnore} on a private field with a public {@code getX()} still
1580    * expose {@code x} when field visibility excludes the field.
1581    */
1582   @SuppressWarnings({
1583      "java:S135" // Two continues in inner loop: skip fields without @BeanIgnore or without ignoreAccessors
1584   })
1585   private Set<String> findSuppressedPropertyNamesFromIgnoredFields(PropertyNamer propertyNamer) {
1586      var s = new HashSet<String>();
1587      var ap = config.getAnnotationProvider();
1588      for (var c2 : classHierarchy.get()) {
1589         for (var x : c2.getDeclaredFields()) {
1590            if (! x.isNotStatic() || ! ap.has(BeanIgnore.class, x))
1591               continue;
1592            if (! fieldBeanIgnoreIgnoresAccessors(x))
1593               continue;
1594            var name = ap.find(x).stream()
1595               .filter(x2 -> x2.isType(BeanProp.class) || x2.isType(Name.class))
1596               .map(BeanMeta::name)
1597               .filter(Objects::nonNull)
1598               .findFirst()
1599               .orElse(propertyNamer.getPropertyName(x.getName()));
1600            name = resolveBeanFieldPropertyName(x, name, propertyNamer);
1601            if (nn(name))
1602               s.add(name);
1603         }
1604      }
1605      return s;
1606   }
1607
1608   private static boolean fieldBeanIgnoreIgnoresAccessors(FieldInfo x) {
1609      for (var bi : x.inner().getAnnotationsByType(BeanIgnore.class))
1610         if (bi.ignoreAccessors())
1611            return true;
1612      return false;
1613   }
1614
1615   /*
1616    * Finds a bean field by name in the class hierarchy.
1617    *
1618    * <p>
1619    * Searches through the complete class hierarchy (as defined by {@link #classHierarchy}) to find a field
1620    * with the specified name. The search is performed in the order classes appear in the hierarchy, and the
1621    * first matching field is returned.
1622    *
1623    * <p>
1624    * A field is considered a match if it:
1625    * <ul>
1626    *    <li>Has the exact name specified
1627    *    <li>Is not static
1628    *    <li>Is not transient (unless transient fields are not ignored)
1629    *    <li>Is not annotated with {@link Transient @Transient} (unless transient fields are not ignored)
1630    *    <li>Is not annotated with {@link BeanIgnore @BeanIgnore}
1631    * </ul>
1632    *
1633    * <p>
1634    * This method is used to find fields for bean properties when a field reference is needed but wasn't
1635    * discovered during the initial property discovery phase (e.g., for properties defined only through
1636    * getters/setters).
1637    *
1638    * @param name The name of the field to find.
1639    * @return The {@link FieldInfo} for the field if found, or <jk>null</jk> if no matching field exists
1640    *    in the class hierarchy.
1641    */
1642   private Optional<FieldInfo> findInnerBeanField(String name) {
1643      var noIgnoreTransients = ! config.isIgnoreTransientFields();
1644      var ap = config.getAnnotationProvider();
1645
1646      // @formatter:off
1647      return classHierarchy.get().stream()
1648         .flatMap(c2 -> c2.getDeclaredField(
1649            x -> x.isNotStatic()
1650               && (x.isNotTransient() || noIgnoreTransients)
1651               && (! x.hasAnnotation(Transient.class) || noIgnoreTransients)
1652               && ! ap.has(BeanIgnore.class, x)
1653               && x.hasName(name)
1654         ).stream())
1655         .findFirst();
1656      // @formatter:on
1657   }
1658}
